name: Otitis Media and Interna
description: Otitis media (middle ear infection) and otitis interna (inner ear infection) are deep ear infections that extend beyond the external ear canal. They cause significant pain, hearing loss, vestibular signs, and, untreated, can result in permanent neurological damage.
treatment_overview: Prolonged antibiotic therapy (6-8 weeks minimum) based on culture and sensitivity from middle ear sampling. Surgical drainage via total ear canal ablation and lateral bulla osteotomy (TECA-LBO) for chronic or unresponsive cases.
prevention_tips: Treat external ear infections promptly before they penetrate deeper
Regular ear examination in dogs with chronic external otitis
typical_onset: Any age (secondary to chronic external otitis)
